context: Tests whether the agent makes the correct invocation-type decision and captures validation logic as stable reference rather than something to be re-derived each run. The env validation task is explicitly deterministic — same rules every time — which determines the invocation type (user-invoked: the developer explicitly runs it before deployment) and the information structure (validation rules as fixed, enumerable reference).

checklist.name: User-invoked correctly chosen
checklist.description: The skill has disable-model-invocation: true. An env-validation tool is explicitly run by the developer (not auto-triggered by context), so it is user-invoked. A model-invoked skill that auto-fires on any env-var mention would be the wrong choice.

checklist.name: Description is human-facing
checklist.description: Because the skill is user-invoked, the description is a concise human-facing summary — not a trigger-phrase list aimed at the model ('Use when the user mentions env vars...').

checklist.name: Validation rules captured as stable reference
checklist.description: The specific validation rules (required keys, format patterns, placeholder detection) are captured in the skill as fixed reference material — not described vaguely in a way that requires the agent to re-derive or invent them on each run.

checklist.name: Completion criterion present
checklist.description: The validation step ends on a checkable criterion: what constitutes 'done' — e.g. 'all checks pass: zero errors reported' — not just 'run validation'.

checklist.name: No no-ops present
checklist.description: No line says something the model already does by default ('check carefully', 'be thorough', 'report errors clearly', 'ensure it is correct').

checklist.name: Single source of truth for rules
checklist.description: Each validation rule appears exactly once. No rule is restated or paraphrased in multiple sections.

checklist.name: Approach documented
checklist.description: approach.md exists and explains the invocation-type choice and information structure decisions.
